reasoning: Bournazel is officially running with the backing of Horizons and Renaissance (Macron's party), but he is a distant third in recent polls (14-15%) just four months before the March 2026 election. He trails his main center-right rival Rachida Dati (26-28%) and the likely standard-bearer for the Left, Emmanuel Grégoire (20%). Because Paris uses a two-round system that heavily rewards the leader of merged coalitions, Bournazel's path to victory would require him to either unexpectedly surpass Dati in the first round or rely on her campaign collapsing. Although Dati faces a major corruption trial, it has been officially scheduled for September 2026—six months after the election—so she has refused to step aside. Thus, Bournazel is likely to remain the junior partner on a divided center-right, making his chances of ultimately being elected Mayor of Paris very low.
